changes_made: Renamed 'k8s_audit_log_integrity_validation_enabled' to 'k8s_audit_log_integrity_check_enabled' for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are generally well-mapped to Kubernetes security controls related to detecting and responding to unauthorized changes. Audit logs and admission controllers are appropriate mechanisms for this control. The naming is mostly consistent with the standards, but a minor change was made to improve clarity.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'check' instead of 'validation' to align with common terminology in K8s security checks
title: Configuration Settings | Respond to Unauthorized Changes
step1_reasoning: The control CM-6 (2) focuses on detecting and responding to unauthorized changes. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that audit logs are enabled and configured to capture unauthorized changes, and that log integrity is validated. Additionally, admission controllers can be used to prevent unauthorized changes by enforcing policies at the time of resource creation or modification.
